proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 77 binary features, at least one has a different objective_response rate when the feature is set vs unset (broad univariate screen).

analyses.code: for c in binary_features: stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df[c], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Of 77 binary features, the most strongly associated with objective_response is feature_013 (resp=0.154 if set vs 0.196 if unset, chi-square p=1.1e-33). At least 7 binary features reach p<1e-3 in this screen, indicating multiple binary predictors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (3-level ordinal, prevalence 35%/50%/15% across levels 0/1/2) has a monotone negative association with objective_response, consistent with a performance-status-like covariate.

analyses.code: logit(y ~ feature_051_linear); also rates per level
analyses.result_summary: Response rates across feature_051 = 0/1/2 are 0.211 / 0.157 / 0.111. Monotone decline; logistic with feature_051 as linear ordinal: beta=-0.375 per level, p=1.7e-93. PS0 vs PS2 chi-square p=3.1e-79.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (binary, prevalence 65%) has a negative main effect on objective_response after adjusting for feature_051 and other strong covariates.

analyses.result_summary: feature_013=1 lowers response from 19.6% to 15.4% unadjusted. In the multivariable logistic with feature_051 and other top covariates, beta=-0.302 (OR ~ 0.74), p=1.9e-34.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (binary, prevalence 25%) has a negative main effect on objective_response, plausibly representing an adverse prognostic marker (e.g., metastatic site or comorbidity).

analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted: response 14.4% (f067=1) vs 17.7% (f067=0). Adjusted multivariable: beta=-0.259 (OR ~ 0.77), p=5e-19.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (continuous, range 0-28, ~28% of patients with value 0) is negatively associated with objective_response (per-SD logistic slope < 0).

analyses.result_summary: Higher feature_011 reduces response. Mean 3.38 in responders vs 3.93 in non-responders (t-test p=2.8e-35). Standardized logistic beta=-0.149 per SD, p=1.8e-32. Patients with feature_011=0 have response 0.193 vs 0.160 for feature_011>0 (chi-square p=3.3e-19).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 (binary, prevalence 28.5%) has a positive main effect on objective_response, indicating a favorable prognostic / treatment-related marker.

analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted response 18.2% (set) vs 16.4% (unset), chi-square p=1.0e-6. Adjusted multivariable beta=+0.130 (OR ~ 1.14), p=1.9e-6.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_007 (binary, prevalence 45%) has a positive main effect on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted response 17.5% vs 16.4% (chi-square p=5.9e-4). Adjusted multivariable beta=+0.080 (OR ~ 1.08), p=9.1e-4.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_039 (binary, prevalence ~50%) has a positive main effect on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted response 17.4% vs 16.4% (chi-square p=3.4e-3). Adjusted multivariable beta=+0.079 (OR ~ 1.08), p=1.1e-3.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_099 (continuous, range 1.7-5.5, mean 3.8 — distribution consistent with serum albumin g/dL) is positively associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_099 in responders 3.821 vs 3.798 in non-responders (t-test p=1.3e-4). Adjusted standardized logistic beta=+0.046 per SD, p=1.2e-4.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_063 (continuous, right-skewed, mean 6.1, max 297 — distribution consistent with an inflammatory/labs marker such as LDH or NLR) is negatively associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: logit(y ~ z(feature_063) + core); also log1p transform
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_063 in responders 5.78 vs 6.15 in non-responders (t-test p=4.2e-4). Adjusted standardized beta=-0.046 per SD, p=4.7e-4. Log-transformed log1p: beta=-0.071, p=1.8e-6 (better fit, suggesting a multiplicative scale).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (continuous, range 0-0.80, mean 0.31 — distribution with a spike at 0 and a long right tail, consistent with a fractional biomarker like PD-L1 TPS) is positively associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main-effect logistic beta=+0.037 per SD, p=2.3e-3. Univariate chi-square between feature_092>median and outcome is non-trivial but the main effect is modest; suggests this feature's importance lies in interactions with other covariates rather than as a pure main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 modifies the effect of feature_092 on objective_response (positive interaction): the per-unit increase in feature_092 is more strongly tied to response in feature_006=1 patients than in feature_006=0.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.126 per SD of feature_092 (added to the f006=1 slope), p=1.6e-6. Direction: feature_006=1 amplifies the positive feature_092 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_007 also modifies the feature_092-response relationship (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.130 per SD, p=6.9e-8. feature_007=1 amplifies the positive feature_092 slope, mirroring feature_006.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_039 also modifies the feature_092-response relationship (positive interaction), suggesting features 006/007/039 belong to a related family of effect modifiers (plausibly a treatment regimen) for which feature_092 is a predictive biomarker.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.126 per SD, p=1.6e-7. Together with h12-h13, feature_092 appears predictive only when feature_006/007/039 are present.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refining h12-h14: features 006, 007, 039 are statistically independent in the data (each pair's pairwise OR ~1; observed all-three-on prevalence 6.4% matches the product 0.285*0.447*0.500=6.4%), so they likely represent three separate features rather than redundant labels of the same underlying construct.

analyses.code: pd.crosstab pairwise plus three-way prevalence vs product of marginals
analyses.result_summary: Pairwise cross-tabs: P(006=1|007=1)=0.287, P(006=1|007=0)=0.283; P(006=1|039=1)=0.285; etc. All conditional prevalences match the marginal 0.285, so feature_006/007/039 are mutually independent. Observed triple prevalence 6.43% vs expected 6.37% under independence.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive 3-way interaction among feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039 on objective_response: the simultaneous presence of all three confers an additional response benefit beyond the sum of the pairwise terms.

analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction term beta=+0.419, p=7.3e-5. Pairwise interaction terms among 006/007/039 are individually small and non-significant (p>0.3), but the triple-way term is strong. Subgroup with all three set has unadjusted response 22.98% vs 16.48% in others.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The combination indicator 'combo' = (feature_006=1 AND feature_007=1 AND feature_039=1) interacts strongly with feature_092: in combo=1 patients, response increases steeply with feature_092, while in combo=0 patients there is no biomarker effect.

analyses.result_summary: combo main-effect beta=+0.268 (p=4.7e-6); combo x z(feature_092) interaction beta=+0.605, p=2.9e-38. The interaction is enormous: in combo=1 patients the per-SD slope is roughly +0.6+0.0=+0.6 vs roughly 0 in combo=0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the high-feature_092 subgroup (>0.6, top ~10%) AND combo=1, the response rate is dramatically elevated relative to all other patients. This identifies a clinically interpretable 'predictive biomarker x treatment' subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: 2x2 stratification: combo=0 & f092 low: resp=0.165 (n=41,971); combo=0 & f092 high: resp=0.162 (n=4,813); combo=1 & f092 low: resp=0.171 (n=2,864); combo=1 & f092 high: resp=0.710 (n=352). The 4-fold elevation is concentrated in the combo=1 + f092>0.6 cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (PS-like) does NOT meaningfully modify the effects of feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, or feature_092 on response (i.e., the treatment-biomarker effect is approximately uniform across PS strata).

analyses.code: logit(y ~ feature_051 * X + core) for X in {f006, f007, f039, f092}
analyses.result_summary: Interaction p-values: feature_051 x feature_006 p=0.29 (beta=+0.042); x feature_007 p=0.14; x feature_039 p=0.34; x feature_092 p=0.84 (beta=-0.004). None significant. PS exerts a strong main effect on response but does not differentially modulate the treatment/biomarker effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 modestly modifies the effect of feature_092 on response: the f092 slope may be steeper in feature_013=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+0.052 per SD, p=0.034. Marginal but suggestive. Sign indicates the (small) f092 main effect is somewhat amplified when feature_013=1, although this is much weaker than the 006/007/039 interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_043 (histology: squamous vs adenocarcinoma) does NOT have a significant effect on objective_response after adjusting for the core covariates.

analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted response 17.4% (squamous) vs 16.7% (adenocarcinoma), chi-square p=0.077. Adjusted: beta=+0.037, p=0.17. Not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (smoking status: current/former/never) does NOT have a significant effect on objective_response after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted resp: current=0.172, former=0.168, never=0.164 (chi-square p=0.27). Adjusted: smk_former vs current beta=+0.004, p=0.88; smk_never vs current beta=-0.002, p=0.96. No significant effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_123 (race: white/black/hispanic/asian/other) does NOT have a clinically meaningful adjusted effect on objective_response in this cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients (vs reference asian): black +0.004 p=0.94; hispanic -0.041 p=0.47; other +0.045 p=0.63; white -0.078 p=0.13. None significant. Unadjusted LRT p=0.047 across all race levels but disappears after adjustment for the core clinical covariates.

analyses.code: logit(y ~ feature_078 + core)  # feature_078 is age-like
analyses.result_summary: feature_078 (continuous, 30-90, mean 65 — likely age in years): adjusted beta=+0.003 per SD, p=0.80. No significant age effect on response after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005 (insurance: medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured) is associated with objective_response after adjustment, with private insurance higher than medicaid (the reference).

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted (reference medicaid): medicare beta=+0.063 p=0.10; private beta=+0.089 p=0.019; uninsured beta=+0.082 p=0.23. Only private is significant; the magnitude (OR~1.09) is small. Most of the apparent insurance differences disappear once feature_051 (PS-like) and other covariates are included.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full multivariable picture: objective_response is driven by (a) a strong negative ordinal effect of feature_051 (PS-like), (b) negative main effects of feature_013, feature_067, feature_011, feature_063, (c) positive main effects of feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, feature_099, feature_092, and (d) a single dominant interaction structure: features 006, 007, 039 act synergistically with each other AND with feature_092 such that the combination feature_006=1 & feature_007=1 & feature_039=1 with feature_092 in the top decile yields response ~0.71. Demographics (age feature_078, race feature_123, smoking feature_057) and histology (feature_043) have no meaningful adjusted effect; insurance (feature_005, private vs medicaid) shows only a small positive signal.

analyses.code: logit(y ~ all features) and AUC; final summary table
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted multivariable model with 10-feature core has training AUC 0.602; adding the (combo x feature_092) interaction raises it to 0.609. The 71% response rate in the combo=1 + f092>0.6 stratum (n=352) is the most striking finding and is robust: in the same f092>0.6 + combo=1 + feature_013=0 cell (n=124) response is 0.726. Overall, the data describe a classical treatment-biomarker dependence: a multi-component treatment indicator (006&007&039) is only effective when a continuous biomarker (feature_092) is sufficiently elevated.
